rationale: Final HH wave (4 books). All four are non-narrative meta-volumes: the 2007 Collected Visions artbook omnibus, both Scripts script-books printing audio-drama production texts, and the 2018 hardback Visions of Heresy re-release. Per the omnibus/collection/scriptbook rule, factions/locations/characters/facets aggregate the constituent works (the four original Visions volumes + Kaban Project for the artbooks; six audio dramas per script volume). | Extended 2026-06-10 (claude-opus-4-8[1m]): appended HH-0295..0297 from the weekly-refresh promotion wave (Brief 133). They fall inside this HH domain-end restbatch's positional slot, so loop:next reported the HH domain complete and never surfaced them; adding them grows 030 to seven books. New three: Zardu Layak: The Crimson Apostle (Word Bearers character study, 2026, unrated), The Shattered and the Soulless (Siege of Terra omnibus), Dropsite Massacre (Isstvan V, John French).

overrides.synopsis: The Horus Heresy: Collected Visions gathers the four original Visions volumes — Visions of War, Darkness, Treachery, and Death — alongside the exclusive short story 'The Kaban Project' by Graham McNeill. Alan Merrett's text threads concept art, illustration, and rulebook panels into a chronological reference for the civil war that breaks the Emperor's Legiones Astartes, from the Great Crusade's triumphs to Horus Lupercal's fall, the purge of Isstvan III, the Drop Site Massacre at Isstvan V, and the long road to the siege of Terra. 'The Kaban Project' shadows Mechanicum adept Pallas Ravachol on Mars as he uncovers a sentient war-machine commissioned for the Warmaster and is hunted across the Red Planet for knowing it lives.

This script collection prints the production texts behind the first six Black Library Horus Heresy audio dramas, with editor Christian Dunn's introduction, soundtrack cues, and character notes. Readers follow Konrad Curze hunting deserters in 'The Dark King,' Rogal Dorn arguing with Sigismund atop the Lightning Tower over Terra, Corvus Corax leading the broken Raven Guard out of the Isstvan V trap in 'Raven's Flight,' and Nathaniel Garro's renegade Death Guard in 'Oath of Moment' and 'Legion of One' as they take ship from Isstvan back toward Terra. 'Butcher's Nails' rides with Angron and Kharn through a World Eaters compliance, the implants ever louder. A volume for readers who already know the audios and want the bones beneath the sound.

The second script collection prints the production texts behind six further Horus Heresy audio dramas, with editor Laurie Goulding's introduction, soundtrack cues, and character notes. Malcador the Sigillite shapes a quiet war on Terra in 'The Sigillite.' Captain Garro returns three times — 'Sword of Truth' and 'Burden of Duty' continue his Knight-Errant arc, while 'Honour to the Dead' tracks a haunted Death Guard frigate. 'Grey Angel' sends an Inquisitor and a Knight-Errant chasing rumours of one of the Lion's sons. 'Censure' drops Aeonid Thiel and a handful of Ultramarines onto a fractured world to hunt a Word Bearers preacher dug in among the rubble. A reference volume for listeners who want the page behind the audio.

The 2018 hardback Visions of Heresy re-presents the four original art volumes — War, Darkness, Treachery, Death — and the short story 'The Kaban Project' by Graham McNeill in the layout of Forge World's Heresy black-books. Alan Merrett's original text, revised and extended by Guy Haley, threads concept art, illustration, and new Neil Roberts pieces into a chronological reference for the civil war that breaks Horus Lupercal's loyalty and shatters the Emperor's Legiones Astartes — Isstvan III's purge, the Drop Site Massacre at Isstvan V, and the long road to the siege of Terra. 'The Kaban Project' shadows Mechanicum adept Pallas Ravachol as he uncovers a sentient war-machine commissioned for the Warmaster and is hunted across Mars for knowing it lives.

Zardu Layak has been chosen by the gods. Guided by visions of his primarch Lorgar, the Crimson Apostle of the Word Bearers believes he is destined to set the stage for the great rebellion and change the galaxy forever. To fulfil that destiny, he must journey to the forgotten world of Helwain and claim the Anakatis Blades — weapons of terrible and malign power. But Helwain is haunted, and as spectral echoes of his past surround him, Layak is forced to confront the question that broke his Legion's faith: what if the truth is a lie? A character study of devotion, corruption, and the cost of surrender to a dark purpose, tracing how a Space Marine remakes himself into something far more terrible in service to gods who may never be satisfied.

flags.reason: The conflict is internal and supernatural; no opposing loyalist Legion is identified in available sources, so only the Word Bearers are tagged.

overrides.synopsis: An omnibus collecting two novellas set during the Siege of Terra. In Sons of the Selenar, the battered crew of the Sisypheum — survivors drawn from the Iron Hands, Salamanders, and Raven Guard who forged the Shattered Legions after Isstvan — descend to the ravaged surface of Luna following garbled transmissions, only to find themselves ensnared in the ancient secrets of the Selenar genesmiths and the terrifying origins of the Space Marine. In Fury of Magnus, the Crimson King himself comes to the Imperial Palace during the Siege, pursuing a fragment of his own shattered soul into the most heavily defended sanctum on Terra and toward a final reckoning with the Emperor. Together the two novellas form a companion piece to Graham McNeill's Thousand Sons saga and close out the arc of the Sisypheum's survivors.

flags.reason: Two distinct novellas with different foes (the Selenar/Luna horrors in one, the Palace defenders facing Magnus in the other); no single opposing Legion is pinned, so antagonist Legions are left untagged.

overrides.synopsis: When Horus's rebellion is revealed, Rogal Dorn and Malcador the Sigillite dispatch the largest loyalist force ever assembled to crush the uprising before it can spread. Three entire Legions — the Iron Hands, Raven Guard, and Salamanders — descend on Isstvan V expecting to deliver judgement. They do not know that four more Legions wait in orbit, loyal only to the Warmaster. Through the eyes of Iron Hands warrior Castrmen Orth and the Blood Crow Kaedes Nex of the Raven Guard, witness the moment the Imperium breaks. The first wave of loyalists is shattered by treachery, cut down by the very brothers they fought beside across a hundred worlds. On the ash-fields of Isstvan V, the Great Crusade ends and a new age of darkness begins.
